Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated NT-proBNP, suggestive of possible cardiac dysfunction or heart failure. Lipid profile reveals high LDL, low HDL, and elevated iron saturation, indicating increased cardiovascular risk. Hemoglobin is slightly elevated but likely not clinically significant. Other parameters, including renal, liver, and glucose metabolism, are within normal limits. Recommend clinical correlation for cardiac status and cardiovascular risk management.
Provider Organization: St Matthews Health & Community Centre
